tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: OCR
tools.description: This tool can recognize all text on the input image.

outputs.description: OCR results, include bbox in x1, y1, x2, y2 format and the recognized text.

tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription
tools.description: Describe the attribute of a region of the input image.

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

dialogs.content: Determine what organ is shown in the image

dialogs.thought: First orient to modality and anatomy on this axial abdominal MRI to identify the target organ.

content.content: Axial grayscale MRI slice through the upper abdomen. The liver is seen on the left side of the image and the spleen on the right, with both kidneys partially visible posteriorly. Two white arrows point to small structures superior/medial to the upper poles of the kidneys, consistent with adrenal glands.

dialogs.thought: Check for any labels or annotations that might specify the organ or side.

arguments.attribute: Identify the structure indicated by the right-sided arrow and describe its appearance/location relative to the kidney

dialogs.thought: Verify that the arrowed structure is anatomically consistent with an adrenal gland (small, triangular, above kidney).

content.content: Within the boxed region there is a small, low-to-intermediate signal, roughly triangular/curvilinear soft-tissue structure located just superior and slightly medial to the upper pole of the adjacent kidney—appearance and location are consistent with an adrenal gland.

dialogs.content: The image is an MRI of the abdomen, specifically focusing on the adrenal glands, which are small, triangular-shaped glands located on top of both kidneys.
